title: bumelia obtusifolia subsp. excelsa
intro: Bumelia obtusifolia subsp. excelsa, also known as the yellow buckeye or excelsa bumelia, is a sub species of the Bumelia obtusifolia plant, a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bumelia obtusifolia subsp. excelsa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
